name: Abe Kuk

dental_history.notes: History of moderate caries and periodontal issues. Previous RCT #36 with PFM crown. Penicillin allergy — use clindamycin if ABX needed.

clinical_notes:
CLINICAL NOTES — Abe Kuk (DOB: 1997-08-03)
Date: 2026-03-15  Provider: Dr. Nakamura
Chart #: AK-2026-001

ALLERGIES: PENICILLIN (hives) — substitute clindamycin.

PATIENT HISTORY:
- Chronic moderate periodontitis, stabilized after SRP (2024-03).
- RCT #36 completed 2024-11, PFM crown seated 2025-01.
- Previous composite #14 (2023-07) for mesial caries.
- Periodontal maintenance every 4 months since 2024.
- Social history: occasional smoker (5 cig/week), moderate
  alcohol consumption.

CHIEF COMPLAINT: Crown on #36 feels "high" when biting.
Also noticed bleeding gums lower front when flossing.

CLINICAL FINDINGS:
- Tooth #36: PFM crown with high occlusal contact on
  mesio-lingual cusp. Occlusal adjustment needed.
  Percussion negative, no periapical pathology on PA.
  Crown margins intact, no recurrent decay.
- Tooth #14: Previous composite showing marginal staining
  and slight ditching at distal margin. Early secondary
  caries suspected. Recommend replacement restoration (D2392).
- Tooth #24: Mesial caries detected on bitewing radiograph.
  Enamel-dentin junction involvement. Moderate severity.
  Recommend composite restoration (D2391).
- Tooth #31: Facial recession 3mm with root sensitivity.
  Cervical abrasion noted. Recommend desensitizing agent
  and soft-bristle brush education.

PERIODONTAL ASSESSMENT:
- Probing depths: generalized 3-4mm, localized 5mm at
  #31 facial, #36 distal, #46 mesial.
- Bleeding on probing: 25% of sites (improved from 45%).
- Lower anterior lingual: moderate calculus buildup.
- Gingival inflammation localized to #31-41 facial.

TREATMENT PLAN:
1. [IMMEDIATE] Tooth #36 — Occlusal adjustment of PFM crown
2. [SOON] Tooth #14 — Replace composite restoration (D2392)
3. [SOON] Tooth #24 — Composite restoration (D2391)
4. [ROUTINE] Tooth #31 — Desensitizing treatment + patient
   education on brushing technique
5. [ROUTINE] Full mouth — Periodontal maintenance (D4910)

RADIOGRAPHIC NOTES:
Bitewing radiographs (4) and PA #36 taken. #36 RCT fill
adequate, periapical area clear. #14 distal radiolucency
at restoration margin. #24 mesial radiolucency at DEJ.
Generalized mild-moderate horizontal bone loss consistent
with history of periodontitis. No acute pathology.

NOTES: Adjusted occlusion on #36 crown — patient reports
improved bite. Scheduled #14 and #24 restorations for next
visit. Reinforced flossing technique for lower anteriors.
Periodontal maintenance due in 4 months. NOTE: Penicillin
allergy documented — prescribe clindamycin 300mg if
antibiotics needed. Next visit: 3 weeks.
